reasoning: The control 'Customer Data Protection' focuses on ensuring data is not publicly accessible and is protected. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that network policies are in place to restrict ingress and egress traffic, services are not exposed unnecessarily, and secrets are encrypted at rest. NetworkPolicies can be used to ensure that pods are not accessible from the internet by default. Similarly, ensuring that services are not of type NodePort can prevent unintended exposure. Encrypting secrets at rest aligns with protecting sensitive data.
title: Customer Data Protection
